entities.name: Pickleball
entities.description: A racket sport played with two or four players.

entities.name: Racket
entities.description: A smooth-faced paddle used in pickleball.

entities.name: Pickleball ball
entities.description: A perforated, hollow plastic ball used in pickleball.

entities.name: Net
entities.description: A 34-inch-high net used to separate the two sides of the court.

entities.name: Doubles badminton court
entities.description: The type of court used in pickleball, resembling a doubles badminton court.

entities.name: Non-volley zone
entities.description: A 7-foot area on each side of the net where players cannot strike the ball before it has bounced.

entities.name: Tennis
entities.description: A racket sport that pickleball resembles aspects of.

entities.name: Table tennis
entities.description: A racket sport that pickleball resembles aspects of.

entities.name: Pickle boat
entities.description: A type of boat where oarsmen were chosen from leftovers, possibly the origin of the game's name.

entities.name: Dog Pickles
entities.description: The family dog that may have influenced the naming of pickleball.

entities.name: Pickleball court
entities.description: A rectangular playing surface for pickleball.

entities.name: Ace
entities.description: A serve that is not returned by the receiver.

entities.name: APP or APP Tour
entities.description: The official tour for pickleball players.

entities.name: Approach shot
entities.description: A shot executed while moving from the backcourt towards the non-volley line.

entities.name: Around-the-post (ATP)
entities.description: A legal shot that travels outside the net posts.

entities.name: At the net
entities.description: A player positioned at the non-volley line.

entities.name: Attackable ball or Attackable shot
entities.description: A ball returned over the net that allows the receiving side to make a strategic offensive shot.

entities.name: Backcourt
entities.description: The area of the court located near the baseline.

entities.name: Backhand shot
entities.description: A shot made with the palm of the hand facing away from the net.

entities.name: Backswing
entities.description: The backward movement of the paddle in advance of striking the ball.

entities.name: Bagel
entities.description: A shutout game ending when one team earned no points.

entities.name: Bainbridge Cup
entities.description: An international pickleball competition organized by the International Pickleball Federation.

entities.name: Ball! or Ball on!
entities.description: A call made to alert all players when an errant ball is on the court.

entities.name: Ball type
entities.description: The type of pickleball used in a match, either indoor or outdoor.

entities.name: Banger
entities.description: A player that hits mostly powerful drive shots.

entities.name: Baselines
entities.description: The lines parallel to the net at the back of the pickleball court.

entities.name: Bash
entities.description: A hard shot that hits the top of the net and then lands in play on the opponent's side.

entities.name: Bert
entities.description: An approach shot where a player crosses in front of their partner to execute an attack on their partner's side.

entities.name: Bevel
entities.description: One of the eight sides of a standard eight-sided racket handle.

entities.name: Block shot or Blocking
entities.description: A backhanded defensive shot with little or no backswing intended to slow the ball and drop it in the opponent's non-volley zone.

entities.name: Body shot, Body bag, or Tag
entities.description: A shot that hits the body of an opposing player, often intentionally.

entities.name: Bounce it!
entities.description: In doubles, a call made by one partner to the other instructing them to allow the ball to bounce before striking the ball.

entities.name: Carry
entities.description: Hitting the ball in such a way that it does not bounce away from the paddle but tends to be carried.

entities.name: drop shot
entities.description: A soft shot in some racket sports, particularly effective near the net.

entities.name: pickleball
entities.description: A sport played with a paddle and a plastic ball on a badminton court.

entities.name: non-volley zone
entities.description: An area in pickleball where players cannot volley the ball, forcing them to hit it upward.

entities.name: dink shot
entities.description: A very soft drop shot performed near or at the non-volley line.

entities.name: drop volley
entities.description: A drop shot made before the ball bounces, similar to a regular drop shot but with a high arc.

entities.name: dink volley
entities.description: A dink shot made before the ball bounces.

entities.name: Pickleball
entities.description: A sport played with a paddle and a small ball on a badminton court.

entities.name: Paddle
entities.description: The implement used to hit the pickleball in the game.

entities.name: Ball
entities.description: A small, hard ball used in pickleball.

entities.name: Badminton Court
entities.description: The rectangular court on which pickleball is played.
